reasoning: As of early 2025, multiple credible defense and mainstream news outlets (Yonhap, KED Global, Janes, Overt Defense) reported that South Korea and Vietnam were in the "final stages" of negotiating a $276–$300 million government-to-government (G2G) agreement for around 20 to 30 K9 self-propelled howitzers. By January 2025, officials and industry sources noted that the two sides had "largely aligned on the terms," anticipating finalization in the near future. Although some reports (like Army Recognition in March 2025) loosely referred to the deal as already signed, most contemporaneous industry trackers (including Janes) confirm it was still under active final negotiation and set to establish Vietnam as the 11th operator globally. Given the strong mutual incentives—Vietnam's ongoing military modernization and pivot away from Russian defense equipment, combined with South Korea's aggressive and highly successful arms export strategy for the K9—it is highly probable that the deal will reach final government approval. The two-month remaining window (May–June 2025) before the July 1, 2025 deadline provides a reasonably sufficient timeframe for final signatures and the requisite official announcement by DAPA or Vietnam's MND. Therefore, there is a strong likelihood (75%) that the agreement will be officially announced before the resolution date.
